content.full_text:
TO: [x] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[ ] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.
[ ] HELEN   [x] OTHER Mary Ann

FROM: Carla
DATE: 6/27/84
No. T-84-174

[x] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: 86107
INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [x]

CALLER Tina
REPRESENTING Bill Thomas (Lobbyist)
PHONE NUMBER: 441-0992

QUESTION: Bill Thomas and others paid $100 toward the purchase of each ticket for another individual to attend a fundraiser. Is this reportable on Thomas' lobbyist report? How should it be reported?

ANSWER: The contribution is reportable on his lobbyist report. He does not need to indicate on the 620 that an intermediary purchased the ticket, but he should keep a copy of his check in his records along with an explanation. I also discussed the intermediary's obligation to properly disclose the true sources of the contribution to the candidate.
